metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: Dominaria Remastered incorporates cards from 27 different sets, creating an unfocused Limited environment with inconsistent archetypes, some powerful synergies, lots of weak rares, and color imbalances. Blue dominates with above-average commons. Splashing is severely limited due to minimal mana-fixing.

color_notes.{U} Blue: Best color in the set. Premium commons (Man-o'-War, Counterspell, Peregrine Drake, Deep Analysis).
color_notes.{R} Red: Strong for aggro or support. Standouts: Chain Lightning, Solar Blast, Coal Stoker, Goblin Matron. Solid burn/creature mix with fatties (Macetail Hystrodon, Avarax).
color_notes.{B} Black: Excellent value color. Terror (best common removal), Phyrexian Rager, Cackling Fiend, Urborg Uprising.
color_notes.{G} Green: Ramp-focused with ZERO removal. Werebear, Wild Growth, Symbiotic Beast, Fa'adiyah Seer.
color_notes.{W} White: Weakest color. Pacifism, Nomad Decoy, Radiant's Judgment, Kjeldoran Gargoyle. Best as support color.

key_gameplay_notes: Expect 99% two-color decks — splashing is severely limited.
Mana fixing options: Terminal Moraine, Lairs (uncommon, -1 mana efficiency), but Crop Rotation is a trap.
Fundamentals matter more than synergy in this unfocused set.
Green has zero removal — don't enter without an interaction plan.
Cycling enables payoffs from both players — opponents can trigger your Lightning Rift / Invigorating Boon.

mechanics.name: Cycling
mechanics.summary: Discard the card to draw a new one. No downside; all cyclers are playable. Enables threshold and payoffs (Lightning Rift, Invigorating Boon).
mechanics.performance: Universal utility. Cycling lands are essential.

mechanics.name: Threshold
mechanics.summary: Creatures improve at 7+ cards in your graveyard. Build via cycling, spellcasting, and combat trades.

mechanics.name: Flicker / ETB
mechanics.summary: Momentary Blink and Whitemane Lion enable value creatures.

mechanics.name: Storm
mechanics.summary: Copy this spell once for each spell cast earlier in the turn. Grapeshot and Empty the Warrens at common.

mechanics.name: Madness
mechanics.summary: Cast from exile when discarded. Niche Dimir subtheme.
mechanics.performance: Light support; not a full archetype.

mechanics.name: Tribal (Goblin / Zombie)
mechanics.summary: Goblin Matron tutors goblins; zombies enable Dralnu's Crusade + Deadapult.

tier_s.name: Izzet Storm
tier_s.strategy: Cast multiple spells per turn for storm payoff. Mana-refund tools and cantrips enable explosive turns.

tier_a.name: Dimir Control
tier_a.strategy: Card draw + removal-based control. Madness subtheme available.

tier_a.name: Rakdos Tribal
tier_a.strategy: Goblin/zombie synergies. Goblin Matron tutors; bombs include Pashalik Mons and Siege-Gang Commander.

tier_a.name: Golgari Midrange
tier_a.strategy: Standard good-stuff. Removal (Terror), card draw, and fatties (Spiritmonger, Call of the Herd).

tier_a.name: Simic Ramp
tier_a.strategy: Ramp into large creatures. Weak interaction; rely on tempo creatures.

tier_b.name: Azorius Flicker
tier_b.strategy: ETB value creatures + flicker effects. Limited redundancy in value creatures.

tier_b.name: Gruul Cycling
tier_b.strategy: Cycling synergy with payoffs (Lightning Rift, Invigorating Boon). Play 15-16 lands; off-color cyclers are acceptable.

tier_b.name: Orzhov Lifegain
tier_b.strategy: 2-for-1 value. Offset life costs with creatures (Cleric, Kjeldoran Gargoyle). No major synergies.

tier_b.name: Izzet Crab
tier_b.strategy: Horseshoe Crab combo with Quicksilver Dagger / Hermetic Study for repeated value. Secondary to Storm.

tier_c.name: Selesnya Threshold
tier_c.strategy: Fill graveyard (7+ cards) to buff creatures. Limited self-mill; rely on cycling, sorceries, and Fa'adiyah Seer.

options.name: Terminal Moraine
options.note: Warped Landscape predecessor; clunky but functional.

options.name: Rith's Grove
options.note: Lair — uncommon, plays at -1 mana efficiency.

general_tips: Prioritize blue for premium commons and flexibility.
Embrace archetype basics — fundamentals matter more than synergy.
Adjust land counts: Gruul cycling can run 15-16; storm decks can trim lower.
Draft interaction early — removal is sparse in some colors (green has none).
Evaluate signposts skeptically: Radha, Tatyova, and Recoil are strong; Dralnu's Crusade and Quicksilver Dagger have narrow synergies.
Note opponent cycling triggers — Invigorating Boon and Lightning Rift trigger on either player's cycle.
Avoid splash temptation — limited mana-fixing punishes greedy builds.
